programme.desc: Pilot episode of the crime drama, starring Amanda Redman as Superintendent Sandra Pullman, a rising star of the Met who is sidelined after a failed hostage rescue. Put in charge of a new department, she is dismayed to discover the unit is to be manned by a motley crew of old-school former detectives who know little about modern police procedure. However, when they re-examine the case of a man whose conviction for murder 20 years ago has just been overturned, their combined experience and unorthodox methods bring interesting results. Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and James Bolam play Redman's veteran team, with Jon Finch, Tim Woodwork and Philip Bretherton(n)

programme.title: Panorama

programme.title: Silent Witness

After painstakingly extracting the skeleton encased inside, Nikki and the Lyell team must investigate a suspicious death from 20 years previously. programme.title: The Graham Norton Show

Plus, another bunch of intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.desc: On the 75th Holocaust Memorial Day, Sacha's great aunt, a survivor of the concentration camps, is admitted to hospital. Her health is deteriorating severely, and Sacha is devastated by the realisation that she i unlikely to live through the day. Cameron is suspicious of a stabbing victim's boyfriend, only to discover he is hiding injuries of his own. Nicky has to step in when her mother receives threatening phone calls after failing to pay her debts(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part two of two. The concrete-encased remains have been identified and the victim's family tracked down - but the team soon has another suspicious death on their hands when Clarissa's investigation leads her to a cryogenics facility, where a body has been frozen. A post-mortem is required to discover how the victim died and who is responsible for having the remains put on ice(n)

programme.title: Young, Sikh and Proud
programme.desc: Journalist Sunny Hundal explores the legacy of his brother Jagraj Singh, who abandoned a Westernised lifestyle in favour of becoming a prominent Sikh leader, whose activisim sparked an unprecedented revival of young people returning to the faith. Following Jagraj's death from cancer, Sunny reflects on their troubled relationship and examines the rise in religious activism in Sikh culture(n)

programme.title: Easy Ways to Live Well
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Steph McGovern reveal some of the easy fixes that can help to dramatically improve people's diet, fitness and mental wellbeing. In the first edition, Hugh and Steph challenge one GPs' surgery to test out solutions for their compulsive snacking. They also tackle their own health concerns, from Hugh's stress to Steph's sensitive stomach(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crime - Are We Tough Enough?
programme.desc: Lawyers Chris Daw and Ayesha Nayyar spend time with police officers on the streets, finding out what is really happening amid Britain's knife-crime crisis(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell view a chalet bungalow in East Peckham, Kent, a piece of land in Tredegar, south-east Wales, and an end-terrace in Hartshill, Warwickshire(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Beth Hood wants to start a new life for her family down under, but her husband Ben is not convinced, so they decide to spend a trial week living in Perth, Australia(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Staff fear for their lives when an armed man bursts into a shop, and a family business finds itself under attack by a former employee demanding money(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant help two teams at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, Powys, while Charlie Ross finds out about a collection of medieval church paintings(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Medical drama series set in a busy Midlands surgery. This time, a missing phone leads Bear down a miserable rabbit hole(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Tensions rise to deadly levels when a performance troupe of anarchists arrive in Kembleford, leaving Mrs McCarthy unimpressed and Bunty inspired(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is in Cornwall, on hand to help a couple from London make their first joint property purchase with a budget of £750,000. Margherita also makes time to help out the head gardener at Trebah, one of the Great Gardens of Cornwall(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two dedicated farming families hope to impress potential buyers at The Scottish Game Fair with farm produced potato vodka and a selection of wild game burgers(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper scour Lancashire and Cumbria for items to sell at auction in Runcorn. On the way, Louise learns about the method for making Lancashire Cheese(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Matt Baker and Alex Jones(n)

programme.title: Easy Ways to Live Well
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Steph McGovern challenge staff at a garden centre in the Wirral to test remedies for their aches and pains. The pair also tackle their own health concerns, including Steph's apprehension at exercising while pregnant and Hugh's worry that he's starting to lose his mental sharpness. Hugh then meets a family in Devon struggling with a very common ailment - fussy eating. With mealtimes a battlefield, he wants to discover if a counterintuitive idea may finally bring peace to the dinner table(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various creatures go 'undercover' in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. This time, cameras follow Japanese macaques in the snow-covered mountains of Japan, as they gather at steaming hot thermal pools to bathe and socialise. Elsewhere in the cool mountains of Mexico, cameras capture a spectacular gathering of billions of monarch butterflies, and in Bavaria, Germany, Spy Beaver gains unique access to the secretive world of the beaver(n)

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Former couples are reunited to talk about their break-ups. Chantelle wants Tevin to forgive her for sleeping with his best friend, and Rachel tries to win back Niraj(n)

programme.title: My Mate's a Bad Date
programme.desc: Oloni helps Dami, a 24-year-old with a history of dating failures, trying to help him own up to his disastrous habits(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth browse the emporiums of north-east England, where Angus buys an expensive pen and Steven plumps for a loving jug. They then head to the deciding auction in Dundee(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Making a living in farming today is hard work and taking big risks can help a farm survive. Here, three inspirational farming families are celebrated for their ideas and ingenuity, but only one can be crowned Diversification Farmer of the Year at the Farmers Weekly Awards held in London. Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the last programme of knockout week, the second group of five face two tough tests. First they are given three hours, with limited equipment and facilities, to produce a knockout plate that showcases their versatility and style. This dish is judged by a group of diners specialising in the street food and pop-up industry - and the contestant that wins the most votes sails through to the semi-finals. The other four are left to produce a showstopper dish in just 90 minutes - hoping it will be enough to get them through(n)

programme.title: This Week in Parliament
programme.desc: Highlights of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day at Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: Roger and Margaret want to leave the winter in Liverpool behind and rent a property in southern Spain, with local agent Toni on hand to help them. Gloria Hunniford narrates(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of dishes using game. The menu includes a venison dish from Rachel Khoo, Keith Floyd cooking with pheasant recipe, Paul Hollywood and Mary Berry's raised game pie, Raymond Blanc cooking with pigeon, and a rabbit dish from Nigel Slater(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: A double bill of challenges as Eric Knowles faces Ochuko Ojiri at an auction in Colchester, before Kate Bliss and Caroline Hawley square up at an antiques fair in Surrey(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Katherine Downes presents action from day eight of the opening Grand Slam tournament of the year, which takes place at Melbourne Park, and featured fourth-round matches(n)

programme.title: Indian Ocean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Adventurer Simon Reeve sets out to travel around the coastline of the Indian Ocean, journeying by boat, car, train and plane as he takes in 16 countries. He starts on the tip of South Africa, where he dives with ragged-tooth sharks and joins conservationists helping to save the endangered African penguin, before heading into Cape Town, where he has a close encounter with a drug dealer. Moving on, he hitches a boat to Mozambique, where a nervous captain reveals the threat posed by Somali pirates, and completes this leg of his journey in Zanzibar, a tropical island steeped in the long history of the slave trade(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Ushaw in Co Durham, antique experts David Fletcher and James Lewis put a value on the region's antiques and collectibles. David is delighted by a 1950s toy plane, while James finds a 1940s menu from a famous Hollywood restaurant which has been signed by some top film stars. Host Paul Martin visits the Mining Art Gallery in Bishop Auckland to find out why many coal miners were inspired to pick up a paintbrush(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya, Hal Cruttenden, Kerry Godliman and Ebony Rainford-Brent go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a new quest as he travels around South-east Asia, guided by his 1913 Bradshaw's Handbook on a 2,500-mile railway adventure across six countries. Beginning in Hong Kong, the former politician investigates how Britain won the island and Kowloon from China after two mid-19th century wars over the trade in opium, before boarding the island's most famous funicular to the Peak, and straddling a bamboo pole to learn the traditional Cantonese art of noodle-making(n)

programme.title: Holocaust Memorial Day
programme.desc: Seventy five years after the liberation of Auschwitz concentration camp, more than 150 survivors attend a commemoration to mark Holocaust Memorial Day. Through music, poetry and powerful personal testimony, all those who were persecuted by the Nazis, as well as those who were victims of later genocides are remembered. Among those taking part are cellist Sheku Kanneh-Mason accompanied by his brother Braimah, actor Simon Russell Beale, actresses Rebecca Front, Nina Wadia and Georgina Campbell, and the Fourth Choir. Huw Edwards presents(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another second-round match as the Endeavours take on the Junipers and with both teams having won one game and lost another, this is their last chance to stay in the tournament. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the first of the quarter-final matches, two teams of four students compete to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches to progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: The Windermere Children
programme.desc: In August 1945, psychologist Oscar Friedmann and his team of counsellors look after child survivors of the Holocaust who were sent to the Lake District to begin new lives. Friedmann and art therapist Marie Paneth, philanthropist Leonard Montefiore and sports coach Jock Lawrence have four months to help the children reclaim their lives, while waiting for news of their loved ones. Fact-based drama, starring Thomas Kretschmann, Romola Garai, Tim McInnerny and Iain Glen(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Wild Scotland
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan takes a trip home to the island of Mull where he has just one day to film the rare sea eagle. While searching for a nest site, he spots a host of other wildlife(n)

programme.title: The Last Survivors
programme.desc: Documentary compiling the personal testimonies of the remaining Holocaust survivors living in Britain, all of whom were children during their incarceration in the concentration camps. They discuss how these experiences continue to influence their adult lives and why particular memories have stayed with them. The film follows one man on a return journey to Auschwitz, accompanied by his daughter, while another survivor revisits his home town in Germany for the first time since 1946(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Former police officer Huw Murphy and the Harries family go up against each other with their beloved shire horses at the 37th South Wales Shire Horse Society Show(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Ballycastle in Co Antrim, a coastal community that is becoming known for its crafts and artisan food and drink. Margherita Taylor heads to a farm that produces ethical and sustainable produce, Joe Crowley catches up with a couple who make award-winning smoked salmon and dulse seaweed, and Charlotte Smith goes on a red squirrel safari with a school group(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper scour Northern Ireland for items to sell at auction in Belfast, along the way visiting one of the world's oldest distilleries and sampling seaweed(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Farming friends Mark and Sean launch their Herefordshire-inspired crisps at the Droitwich Food Festival, while horticultural pioneer Sophie sells her Juneberry gin(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace presents the first round of semi-finals week. The eight remaining contestants face two challenges, to be judged by Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing. Firstly, they must prepare a dish that captures the moment that triggered their own love of food. After this, they move on to the invention test, in which they must make a flawless vegan meal(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: Dee and Celia want to leave the cold behind and spend part of the winter in Cyprus, so local property expert Julie is on hand to help(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes to keep children happy, including Paul Hollywood's sausage rolls, James Martin's banana split and Lorraine Pascale's pizza recipe(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Double bill. In Sommiers, Paul Hayes caters for the Gallic masses with a 50-piece dinner service, and John Cameron spends big on a huge male torso sculpture. Jonty Hearnden and Danny Sebastian battle for lots at a London auction house. While Jonty finds himself in a world of fashion, Danny muscles in on his territory(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Katherine Downes presents action from day nine of the opening Grand Slam tournament of the year, which takes place at Melbourne Park, featuring the opening quarter-finals(n)

programme.title: Indian Ocean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer's epic journey continues on the coast of Madagascar, where he goes spearfishing with the Vezo people, whose lifestyle is under threat from their own rapid population growth. On Mauritius, he visits slums that show a different side to the tourist paradise, and he ends this leg in the Seychelles, meeting an Englishman who is turning his private island into the world's smallest national park. Simon also joins the Dutch Special Forces, training to board pirate ships operating on the high seas(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Capesthorne Hall in Cheshire, where Anita Manning falls in love with a pair of Pilkington vases and Mark Stacey finds an unusual lightbulb. Paul also goes behind the scenes of Capesthorne to find out what it takes to keep a stately home running(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya, Hal Cruttenden, Kerry Godliman and Ebony Rainford-Brent go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues exploring Hong Kong, riding the MTR metro system and talking to people involved in the current political protests. He learns about Sun Yat Sen, the first president of the Republic of China, who studied medicine in Hong Kong, and in one of the walled villages of the New Territories, he meets a descendant of one of the region's oldest dynasties(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuiness, Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff go on a road trip in a trio of affordable second-hand convertibles. On the test track, Chris puts the new Ariel Atom through its paces, while Freddie goes bungee jumping off a dam in an old Rover(n)

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams present a week of programmes live from the Cairngorms, showcasing wildlife stories recorded across the UK. The first episode features footage of pine martens, red squirrels, golden eagles and crested tits, and reveals what the current state of the mountainous region can reveal about the future of Britain's climate(n)

programme.title: Belsen: Our Story
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the concentration camp, featuring personal accounts from the few remaining survivors and archive footage shot by the British forces that liberated them. Bergen-Belsen was used to hold prisoners evacuated from camps that had fallen to the Allied advance, leading its population to increase to nearly 60,000 by the winter of 1944. Thousands died at the camp from starvation and disease, their bodies left unburied. The British and Canadian forces who discovered the camp were left with no choice but to burn it to the ground(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam tries to teach her daughters to drive, and cannot stop herself from complaining about the conditions on the blockbuster movie she is working on. Comedy-drama, created by and starring Pamela Adlon(n)

programme.title: The Archiveologists
programme.desc: Diane Morgan and Joe Wilkinson affectionately parody a television disco dance championship(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Jason Bell and Osi Umenyiora to look at the latest news ahead of the Super Bowl, which will see Kansas City Chiefs face San Francisco 49ers(n)

programme.title: Monty Don's American Gardens
programme.desc: The horticulturalist explores gardens of early independent America, learning how the history of the South is still very much evident in the places and people he meets. He visits the famous garden where President Thomas Jefferson, one of America's founding fathers, enjoyed spending his time seeking out seeds and plants for his 1,000-ft vegetable garden. From there he heads to Charleston, which boasts some of the most beautiful trees in the world and learns about the life of the plantations there. Later, in Miami, Monty visits one of the most spectacular Renaissance recreations built on marshland(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass investigate a notorious arsenic poisoning in which a distinguished lawyer and Army veteran hanged for the murder of his wife almost 100 years ago(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Newly qualified nurse Chloe is thrown in at the deep end treating a toddler who has difficulty breathing. A nightshift worker is flown in showing similar symptoms to a heart attack(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper scour Northern Ireland for hidden gems in a soft-top beetle, and discover the stories of legendary heroes and a campaigner for social justice(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Cattle farmers and their protegees bring their finest cows to compete at the Driffield Show, hoping to win the much-coveted red rosettes(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam travels to Northern Ireland to see the Giant's Causeway, Belfast murals and Titanic Museum. He also meets locals to talk about the Troubles. With voiceover(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley with a report on the counterfeit contact lenses that can make your eyesight worse, and the fake waste disposal companies that could cost consumers a fortune. Elsewhere, Michelle shows how to identify fake emails and dangerous links online, and reveals how criminals can even embed fake messages into a legitimate text chain on your phone(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day. Includes live coverage of Prime Minister's Questions from midday.(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: Sally and Jan want to swap the cold of London for sunshine in Ibiza, and are shown four rental properties on the Spanish island by local property expert Hattie(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott celebrates dishes from across the Channel, getting inspiration from France, Italy and Spain. The menu includes the Greedy Italians' linguine, James Martin's Spanish sardines, a chicken dish from Raymond Blanc, and a Bake Off masterclass on how to make a classic Greek spanakopita(n)

programme.title: Indian Ocean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The third leg of the adventurer's journey takes him from the south of Kenya to the Horn of Africa. Along the way he joins a Ugandan peace-keeping force in Mogadishu, the capital of war-torn Somalia, which turns out to be the most dangerous leg of his journey as he ends up under fire on the front line. In contrast, he finds neighbouring Somaliland a stable and democratic home to thousands of Somalis fleeing fighting and famine. In Kenya's Tana Delta, he meets villagers facing eviction from an area that the government is turning over to sugar cane production for bio-fuels(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Katherine Downes presents action from day 10 of the opening Grand Slam tournament of the year, which takes place at Melbourne Park, featuring the concluding quarter-finals(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Emma Willis is the latest to take part in the popular genealogy documentary, in which famous faces trace their family trees. The TV presenter, who considers herself a true Brummie, is keen to learn more about her family in Birmingham, tracing back to Victorian times and her hard-working ancestor James Gretton, a local merchant. The Big Brother host then learns that her father's grandmother was born in Ireland. Surprised to hear of this new line of ancestry, Emma heads across to Dublin to find out more - but she is disturbed by what she learns(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Thomas Plant and David Harper are on hand at the Watts Gallery in Surrey to find a selection of objects to take to auction(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya, Hal Cruttenden, Kerry Godliman and Ebony Rainford-Brent test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On this leg of his trip in Thailand, Michael Portillo tries his hand at the art of umbrella-making and learns about northern Thai cookery. He explores the walled and moated city of Chiang Mai, former capital of the Lanna Kingdom before it was annexed by the King of Siam. Travelling south to Lampang, he discovers the former centre of the teak wood business and investigates how successive Thai kings preserved their independence from the rival colonial powers of Britain and France(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest on Brexit with Andrew Neil and guests(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar goes on a ramble that straddles the north and south sides of Calderdale, stopping along the way to sketch and read poetry. She begins in the village of Heptonstall, rambles past Hell Hole and along the canal in Hebden Bridge, before climbing to the Stoodley Pike peace monument(n)

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams investigate rumours that swallows are present in Sussex in January. They also look at why there's been a severe lack of snow in Scotland this winter, and share an incredible spectacle in the form of flocks of brent geese migrating from Northern Canada to the urban streets of Dublin(n)

programme.title: Good Omens
programme.desc: Aziraphale and Crowley's friendship across the ages is revealed, as they are seen in the Garden of Eden, at the time of Noah's Ark, in Jerusalem, Ancient Rome, Arthurian Britain, at Shakespeare's Globe Theatre, in a prison in Revolutionary France, Victorian London, getting mixed up with a Nazi spy ring in the Second World War, and in London's 'Swinging Sixties'. Meanwhile in the present day, Agnes Nutter's descendant arrives on her world-saving mission. Starring Michael Sheen, David Tennant, Adria Arjona and Miranda Richardson(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Last week saw the return of the comedy show in which three comedians take the stage of the Hammersmith Apollo in west London. The premise is simple. One comic acts as host, warming up the audience before introducing short sets by two other stand-ups. Future editions promise the likes of Ed Byrne, Rob Beckett, Joel Dommett, Henning Wehn, Lucy Porter, but tonight Nish Kumar plays host, before handing over to David O'Doherty and Luisa Omielan(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Belsen: Our Story
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the concentration camp, featuring personal accounts from the few remaining survivors and archive footage shot by the British forces that liberated them. Bergen-Belsen was used to hold prisoners evacuated from camps that had fallen to the Allied advance, leading its population to increase to nearly 60,000 by the winter of 1944. Thousands died at the camp from starvation and disease, their bodies left unburied. The British and Canadian forces who discovered the camp were left with no choice but to burn it to the ground(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Mediterranean Voyage
programme.desc: The crew heads down the Croatian coast, where Francesco da Mosto's first challenge is to scale the heights of the main mast to hang the Venetian flag aloft. They drop anchor in the beautiful city of Split where they visit Roman Emperor Diocletian's retirement villa - the oldest lived-in palace in the world(n)

programme.title: A Very British Romance with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian delves into 300 years of love's rituals to uncover the forces shaping the happy ever after, revealing how people's most intimate thoughts and feelings have been affected by social, political and cultural ideas. Lucy begins in the Georgian age, when - after centuries of marriage being as much about business as love - women began to have an unprecedented degree of romantic freedom(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: An exploration of the far-reaching implications of a genome editing technology CRISPR, which opens the door to curing diseases, reshaping the biosphere and allowing people to design their own children. The documentary looks at the revolutionary method through the eyes of the scientists who discovered it, the families it is affecting and the bio-engineers who are testing its limits(n)

programme.title: The Windermere Children: In Their Own Words
programme.desc: The story of the pioneering project to rehabilitate child survivors of the Holocaust on the shores of Lake Windermere, with first-hand testimonies from some of those who attended. Reflecting on the survivors' lives after Windermere, the film includes home-movie footage and footage of their annual reunions(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: David Attenborough has a passion for birds' eggs, which protect the young from the outside world at the same time as allowing them to breathe and are strong enough to withstand the full weight of an incubating parent and weak enough to allow a chick to break free. In this documentary, the broadcaster reveals the secrets of birds' eggs from creation to hatching, exploring how they are made, the reasons behind their shape and their intrinsic purpose(n)

programme.title: Treasures of the Indus
programme.desc: Historian and museum curator Sona Datta explores the story of the Indian subcontinent told through its people, places and dynasties, and looking at their effect on the modern world. She begins by investigating Pakistan's intriguing multicultural past, which also produced some extraordinary and little-known works of art(n)

programme.title: Gluck: Who Did She Think He Was?
programme.desc: Profile of the 1930s high society painter, whose prestigious shows were popular with royalty and celebrities, and who also dressed as a man and had a number of female lovers(n)

programme.title: The Windermere Children: In Their Own Words
programme.desc: The story of the pioneering project to rehabilitate child survivors of the Holocaust on the shores of Lake Windermere, with first-hand testimonies from some of those who attended. Reflecting on the survivors' lives after Windermere, the film includes home-movie footage and footage of their annual reunions(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world. With Katty Kay and Christian Fraser(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Eric and Bobby conclude their journey around the United Kingdom by heading to Scotland, where they visit the Edinburgh birthplace of 19th-century scientist James Clerk Maxwell, whose pioneering work includes the 1865 theory of electromagnetism. The duo then analyse the science behind the deep-fried Mars Bar before heading to Glasgow explore the Hunterian Museum's collection of antiquarian experimental instruments and delve into the personal correspondence of mathematical physicist and engineer Lord Kelvin(n)

programme.title: London: A Tale of Two Cities with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: The historian examines how London coped with a series of disastrous events in the 17th century, including the Great Fire, the Plague and the Civil War, and became one of Europe's most powerful cities. He uses information from surveys taken by chroniclers at the beginning and end of the century to explore the qualities that enabled Londoners to thrive, and explains how events of the period laid the foundations for the English capital's success(n)

programme.title: Europe: Them or Us
programme.desc: Part one of two. Nick Robinson explores the troubled history of the UK's relationship with Europe. The first programme examines why British governments initially shunned the new Common Market then begged to join it. It also explores the decisive part played by three British prime ministers, Winston Churchill, Harold Macmillan and Edward Heath(n)

programme.title: Europe: Them or Us
programme.desc: Part two of two. Nick Robinson goes behind the closed doors of Whitehall and Brussels to hear from the decision-makers about Britain's relationship with the EEC, now EU. It examines the way the first European referendum was fought in 1975 and the changes in the European project since then. The film features contributions from four British prime ministers - Margaret Thatcher, John Major, Tony Blair and David Cameron. There is also footage of Tony Benn, Nick Clegg, Jacques Delors and Iain Duncan Smith(n)

programme.title: Border Country: When Ireland Was Divided
programme.desc: Since the border between the UK and Ireland was created in 1922, film crews and journalists have descended there to try and make sense of its absurdities and contradictions, as well as the turmoil it can cause. This programme showcases archival footage from the past 100 years, together with the stories of people whose lives have been affected by the crucial dividing line between the UK and Ireland(n)

programme.title: Britain and the Sea
programme.desc: Broadcaster David Dimbleby sails his yacht Rocket around the British coastline, exploring different aspects of the nation's maritime history, art and architecture along the way. He begins by sailing the south-west coast of Devon and Cornwall, where he tells the story of Sir Francis Drake and an array of adventurers, explorers, pirates and smugglers. He also welcomes record-breaking solo yachtswoman Ellen MacArthur on board and learns about one of the oldest maritime art forms of all - the tattoo, before agreeing to have one himself(n)

programme.title: How to Get Ahead
programme.desc: Documentary in which writer and broadcaster Stephen Smith finds out what it took to survive and prosper in the most artistic and decadent royal courts in history. He begins with Richard II, who cultivated the first truly artistic court in England. Painters, sculptors, poets, tailors, weavers and builders flocked there to make their fortunes, but these were dangerous times, and getting close to Richard brought many to a sticky end. Featuring contributions by David Tennant and Clarissa Dickson-Wright(n)

programme.title: Britain's Most Fragile Treasure
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez explores the complex history of York Minster's Great East Window, the largest medieval stained-glass window in England. Composed of 311 panels, it was originally designed to tell the complete biblical history of the world from creation to the Last Judgement, and has been compared to Michelangelo's work at the Sistine Chapel. The historian closely examines the handiwork of its creator John Thornton, revealing intricate details that would not normally be visible, and follows a project to restore the window to its original glory(n)

programme.title: London: A Tale of Two Cities with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: The historian examines how London coped with a series of disastrous events in the 17th century, including the Great Fire, the Plague and the Civil War, and became one of Europe's most powerful cities. He uses information from surveys taken by chroniclers at the beginning and end of the century to explore the qualities that enabled Londoners to thrive, and explains how events of the period laid the foundations for the English capital's success(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Handmade
programme.desc: Filmed in an industrial forge lit only by the welding torch and burning metal, this programme examines the process involved in the crafting of a steel knife. Bladesmith Owen Bush uses a variety of techniques, both ancient and modern, and takes his time over the complex skill of shaping and blending the steel into a knife(n)

programme.title: World's Busiest Railway 2015
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Anita Rani, Robert Llewellyn and John Sergeant go behind the scenes at Mumbai's Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus, revealing the science, systems and staff responsible for keeping this supersized transport system running to schedule. In the opening edition, Dan, Anita and Robert experience conditions known as 'super dense crush load' as they ride on the world's busiest commuter trains, while John heads to Darjeeling's steam-powered hill railway to examine the historical connection between tea and trains(n)

programme.title: The Brexit Storm: Laura Kuenssberg's Inside Story
programme.desc: With Westminster in crisis and the Prime Minister striving to get her deal through Parliament, the BBC's political editor takes viewers through every twist and turn of the most extraordinary political story of recent times. Cameras follow key politicians as well as Laura herself as she reports on exceptional events, from Theresa May's doomed efforts to secure backing for her Chequers plan to cabinet resignations, backbench plots and historic parliamentary defeats, as well as a national campaign for a second referendum(n)

programme.title: The Brexit Storm Continues: Laura Kuenssberg's Inside Story
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the tumultuous events of Boris Johnson's Conservative government. The programme follows his appointment as leader of the Conservative party, attempts to secure a Brexit deal with the European Union, the attempted proroguing of parliament, an act subsequently declared unlawful, and the eventual calling of a general election. Featuring contributions from MPs on all sides, who reveal the pressure they have come under from an increasingly divided and frustrated electorate(n)

programme.title: Art, Passion & Power: The Story of the Royal Collection
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the history of the Royal Collection - the dazzling array of art and decorative objects collected by kings and queens over the past 500 years, and today owned by the Queen as sovereign. The first programme focuses on the works acquired by the collection's founders - Henry VIII and Charles I. Henry VIII tried to overwhelm with magnificence, believing that great art projected power. For Charles I art was a way to compete with other kings through taste, and Andrew reveals how this doomed monarch became the greatest royal collector in British history(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Investigates
programme.desc: It has been described as one of the biggest scandals in the Welsh NHS since devolutio, with parents and babies being failed for years at two hospitals run by the Cwm Taf Morgannwg University Health Board. In this BBC Wales Investigates programme, Wyre Davies examines why patients were put at risk time and time again - and why so far no one has been held to account(n)

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Teenagers messing around in a stolen car collide with a pillar in a multistorey car park. The joyriders escape, but within the smashed concrete, the unmistakable form of a skull is revealed. After painstakingly extracting the skeleton encased inside, Nikki and the Lyell team must investigate a suspicious death from 20 years previously. programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway is joined by two guides who have both used walking to assist them on their very different but, personal journeys. Musician Jules Peters, who fought breast cancer, takes Derek on her favourite walk in Wales, a stunning section of the Llyn coastal path that ends at a famous beach bar. On the Welsh border in Presteigne, Derek meets Tom Davies who walked the entire 1,100-mile perimeter of Wales to raise awareness and funds for Alzheimer's disease. Tom takes Derek on a small section of his welsh wander, which includes the Offa's Dyke path(n)

programme.desc: Daniel becomes the new resident doctor inside the prison where Jimmi is being held, while Bear is behaving mysteriously and an old friend of Al's claims to have seen a ghost(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A man suspected of killing a police officer is seen near Kembleford and Mallory is determined to catch him, but he has to ask the sleuthing priest for help when disaster strikes(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Dorset to help a couple with £475,000 find a rural property that fits the bill when it comes to a workshop and providing space to keep hens. Jules also delves in the county's glove-making heritage and produces his own bespoke pair(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Farming friends Mark and Sean launch their Herefordshire-inspired crisps at the Droitwich Food Festival, while horticultural pioneer Sophie sells her Juneberry gin(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper scour Northern Ireland for items to sell at auction in Belfast, along the way visiting one of the world's oldest distilleries and sampling seaweed(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Getaways
programme.desc: Holly Hamilton and JJ Chalmers report on a dual-centre city break in the Czech Republic. They start out in the capital, Prague, before heading to Brno, the country's second city. Highlights include a river tour, a wall dedicated to the Beatles, one of the world's ugliest buildings and an art gallery with its very own airship. For the short break, actor Kristian Nairn, better known to Game of Thrones fans as gentle giant Hodor, visits Hillsborough Castle in Northern Ireland's County(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Leo continues to cause trouble on the market, but he soon regrets abusing Kat when she hits back at him and attacks his stall. Linda is upset when Mick does not stand up for her against gossips, the Panesars' mother Suki arrives in Walford and makes an immediate impression on Jean as she orders her sons about and Ian revels in his councillor win(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Hundreds of miles of motorways are being made smart by turning the hard shoulder into a live lane, but many experts think they are dangerous for cars that have broken down in them. Richard Bilton speaks to the families of crash victims and asks whether Britain's motorways are becoming death traps(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Teenagers messing around in a stolen car collide with a pillar in a multistorey car park. The joyriders escape, but within the smashed concrete, the unmistakable form of a skull is revealed. After painstakingly extracting the skeleton encased inside, Nikki and the Lyell team must investigate a suspicious death from 20 years previously. Meanwhile, Clarissa faces a difficult decision regarding her mother's care. First of a two-part episode of the crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, David Caves, Richard Lintern and Liz Carr(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary following the wedding preparations of Belfast woman Janine Howard, who was left with severe facial disfigurement after contracting a rare flesh-eating infection when she was a child(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: People said to be able to cure a host of common ailments using secret prayers and actions, meeting Sr Nora Smyth, Pat McGrade and Danny Gallagher to discover how this still prevails in a contemporary society(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam is back at his parents' house in Leeds intending to prepare for a performance at a local spoken-word night, but instead procrastinates and plays old video games. But when he turns on his childhood computer and stumbles on an old home-made hip-hop track he created with his teenage mates, it triggers a memory(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Outspoken funnyman Frankie Boyle hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of humour before handing over to the acerbic Simon Evans and actress/stand-up Aisling Bea(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Robert Downey Jr and Emma Thompson, stars of the big-screen fantasy adventure Dolittle, while Hugh Laurie discusses his role in The Personal History of David Copperfield. Monty Python's Terry Gilliam chats about his film The Man Who Killed Don Quixote, while American singer-songwriter Sara Bareilles performs She Used to Be Mine, from her musical Waitress. Plus, another bunch of intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crime - Are We Tough Enough?
programme.desc: Chris Daw QC and Ayesha Nayyar look at the role of prisons in the justice system, examining the values of using incarceration for both deterrence and rehabilitation(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the sales of a terrace house in Hornchurch, a piece of land in Newquay, and a commercial space in Rochdale(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Mother-of-triplets Sarah thinks life would be easier in Australia to raise her little girls. However, stay-at-home father Conor has major concerns. A trial week in Perth gives the pair the chance to experience what life could offer them - but is a move to Oz the right decision for this family's future? Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Featuring a disabled woman whose carer is stealing from her and a theatre studies graduate facing a real-life drama when a hammer-wielding robber attacks the shop where she works(n)

programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Anglesey's Mona Showground where a married couple take on newlyweds, with Caroline Hawley and Philip Serrell on hand for expert advice(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a calligrapher from East London find a new home in Devon on a budget of £400,000, and attends a sea shanty festival on the southern coast(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Cattle farmers and their protegees bring their finest cows to compete at the Driffield Show, hoping to win the much-coveted red rosettes(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper scour Northern Ireland for hidden gems in a soft-top beetle, and discover the stories of legendary heroes and a campaigner for social justice(n)

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kush is racked with guilt over Leo but Kat is adamant he cannot tell the truth. Linda promises Mick she does not need to go to rehab and agrees to ration her drinking - but temptation proves too much. Keegan is humiliated when his contract at Gray's office is terminated, and when Suki persuades Kheerat to take an office nearby, it's clear she is planning something(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: On the 75th Holocaust Memorial Day, Sacha's great aunt, a survivor of the concentration camps, is admitted to hospital. Her health is deteriorating severely, and Sacha is devastated by the realisation that she i unlikely to live through the day. Cameron is suspicious of a stabbing victim's boyfriend, only to discover he is hiding injuries of his own. Nicky has to step in when her mother receives threatening phone calls after failing to pay her debts(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part two of two. The concrete-encased remains have been identified and the victim's family tracked down - but the team soon has another suspicious death on their hands when Clarissa's investigation leads her to a cryogenics facility, where a body has been frozen. A post-mortem is required to discover how the victim died and who is responsible for having the remains put on ice(n)

programme.title: The Search
programme.desc: Return of the programme following rescue teams in Northern Ireland. A major search operation is launched when a man in his 40s is reported missing near the north coast, while in Belfast volunteers are called out to save a man who has fallen into the river.(n)

programme.title: Young, Sikh and Proud
programme.desc: Journalist Sunny Hundal explores the legacy of his brother Jagraj Singh, who abandoned a Westernised lifestyle in favour of becoming a prominent Sikh leader, whose activisim sparked an unprecedented revival of young people returning to the faith. Following Jagraj's death from cancer, Sunny reflects on their troubled relationship and examines the rise in religious activism in Sikh culture(n)

programme.title: Easy Ways to Live Well
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Steph McGovern reveal some of the easy fixes that can help to dramatically improve people's diet, fitness and mental wellbeing. In the first edition, Hugh and Steph challenge one GPs' surgery to test out solutions for their compulsive snacking. They also tackle their own health concerns, from Hugh's stress to Steph's sensitive stomach(n)

With mealtimes a battlefield, he wants to discover if a counterintuitive idea may finally bring peace to the dinner table(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various creatures go 'undercover' in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. This time, cameras follow Japanese macaques in the snow-covered mountains of Japan, as they gather at steaming hot thermal pools to bathe and socialise. Elsewhere in the cool mountains of Mexico, cameras capture a spectacular gathering of billions of monarch butterflies, and in Bavaria, Germany, Spy Beaver gains unique access to the secretive world of the beaver(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh asks the questions as contestants Danielle, Anthony, Gareth and Sue pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Graham's body is discovered, the police tell Rhona about his murder, and Rishi fears for someone close(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Daniel discovers Bertie barely conscious in his cot and urges Peter to call an ambulance. When Charlie reveals she's moving into the Rovers, a stressed Tim begs Abi to help him come up with a way to get rid of her. And Maria insists Gary should go to Bristol without her(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show
programme.desc: As January, the toughest month for many people, continues, the financial journalist and Angellica Bell look at what solutions there might be for cutting mounting debts, including comparing the pros and cons of loans and credit cards(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: As Beth rails at Daniel for endangering Bertie's life, he hangs his head in shame. In an attempt to get the babies off to sleep, Chesney suggests to Gemma that they take them for a walk, and Tim tries to convince Charlie she's wasted in the Rovers(n)

programme.title: Cold Feet
programme.desc: Adam reaches out to Laura, but risks upsetting his family as a result, while Karen and Jenny's weekend away is hijacked by their mothers. Pete's attempts to reunite the Three Amigos ends in disaster. John Thomson, James Nesbitt, Fay Ripley, Hermione Norris and Robert Bathurst star in Mike Bullen's long-running comedy drama, with Sacha Parkinson, Gemma Jones, Clare Keelan and Sunetra Sarker(n)

programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents(n)

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: Major documentary telling the inside story of the royal family and the Queen's record-breaking reign. The first episode focuses on the conflict between love and duty that faces the royals - and which has occasionally threatened to bring down the House of Windsor. Edward VIII fatefully chose love when he abdicated to marry divorcee Wallis Simpson. Charles chose duty when he married Diana. The Queen, however, has managed to find a balance, maintaining a long marriage while becoming the longest-serving monarch(n)

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Action from the latest edition of the weekly AEW event, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley(n)

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The celebrated chef arrives in the Dordogne, famous for its traditional hearty cuisine, buried treasures and a love of all things duck. Exploring the region, James tastes three local specialities all cooked for him by the Auberge owner, British ex-pat Chris. After lunch, Chris hunts for another local icon, the black truffle, which he intends to use in his next recipe(n)

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: After a vital discovery at the farm, Stan and Mick feel closer to shoring up their own suspicions about Jeremy. The arrival of Brett, a friend from New Zealand (played by Game of Thrones star Alfie Allen), increases tensions between Jeremy and Julie, while Ann grows ever more alarmed and suspicious of her cousin Jeremy's behaviour, and his handling of the family estate. As preparations for the inquest continue, Stan is concerned that if the bodies are released for cremation, vital evidence will be lost for ever(n)

programme.desc: The Chase presenter Bradley Walsh and his son Barney return for a second series as they embark on another road trip across America together. Brad soon realises that they are not just there to spectate on the sport, they are also expected to take part. However, a nasty fall from an angry bull means Brad spends the rest of the night in hospital(n)

programme.desc: The funerals approach, and without the answers he needs and still more questions being thrown up from White House Farm, Stan's efforts remain fruitless. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Sally, Mark, Ellis and Jo pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah is shocked when confronted by Charity, who struggles to process everything. A decision is made regarding the future(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: When Maria suffers a dizzy spell and tells Ali she's bleeding, he insists on driving her to hospital for a scan. Gemma and Chesney are furious when they realise they've been removed from a marketing campaign, and Daniel thanks Bethany for helping him through such a terrible ordeal(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The Chase presenter Bradley Walsh and his son Barney return for a second series as they embark on another road trip across America together. This time, the actor and his son arrive in Ohio, where they head for Dayton - the birthplace of aviation - before the pair attend a local rodeo later that evening. Brad soon realises that they are not just there to spectate on the sport, they are also expected to take part. However, a nasty fall from an angry bull means Brad spends the rest of the night in hospital(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ali struggles to comfort Maria as she reveals how she lost a baby 11 years ago and can't bear to go through it again. Imran advises a furious Gemma that there's nothing she can do about Freshco's advertising decisions. Sarah tells Bethany to tread carefully with Daniel(n)

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: The funerals approach, and without the answers he needs and still more questions being thrown up from White House Farm, Stan's efforts remain fruitless. When all looks lost for Stan and Mick solving the case, a vital witness comes forward. Six-part drama based on the true story of the mass murder of a family in an Essex farmhouse in August 1985, providing a fresh insight into the tragic events. Starring Alfie Allen, Mark Addy and Andrew Frame(n)

Barnaby and Jones are called in to investigate, and the duo discover that intrigue, sexual tension and academic rivalry are rife among the local stargazing community. programme.desc: Rigsby splashes out on a new sports car in the hope of impressing Miss Jones, but a cruel practical joke by Philip convinces him he has run over his feline friend Vienna. Leonard Rossiter and Frances de la Tour star(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Mr Roper decides he will not allow the flatmates to have a party. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police are called in to investigate when a local judge is the target of a series of mysterious attacks, but the case takes a sinister turn when the prime suspect is found dead on his own doorstep. Peggy is delighted when David's new radio starts picking up police broadcasts, but a news reporter doesn't share her enthusiasm. Rural drama, with Gwen Taylor and David Lonsdale(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Tates fight for a life on the roadside while an idea is fermenting for Turner. Rachel has a suggestion for Christopher, and Luke's fate continues to hang in the balance(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel plans to get Chris out and about, Frank's illness throws a shadow over his future with Kim, Luke continues to languish behind bars, and Pollard plans a big shock for Betty(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly asks Paula round for a meal, Bet tracks Raquel down to a pub where she has been working, while Liz and Jim leave for a holiday in Ireland(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly is horrified to see Paula and Andy leaving number 11 together in the morning. Deirdre is delighted when Samir lets his plane leave without him(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The family plans a party to celebrate Gerry turning 13, but the occasion is disrupted by a swarm of mosquitoes and the birthday boy's frustrations at his mother and siblings not realising that he is growing up. Margo notices his bad mood and takes steps to save the situation, while Louisa worries about Spiros, who has locked himself in a room to sing sad love songs(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police are called in to investigate when a local judge is the target of a series of mysterious attacks, but the case takes a sinister turn when the prime suspect is found dead on his own doorstep. Peggy is delighted when David's new radio starts picking up police broadcasts, but a news reporter doesn't share her enthusiasm. Rural drama, with Gwen Taylor and David Lonsdale(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When an unpopular employee at a military psychiatric hospital is found murdered, there is no shortage of suspects, but a colleague of the man, an exiled Polish Jew, holds himself responsible for the death. Foyle is convinced there is another explanation, but his priorities shift when news of a missing boy from London reaches Hastings. Starring Michael Kitchen, Phyllida Law and Nicholas Woodeson(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A man is found shot dead in a hotel room and the cash he was carrying has gone missing. Ronnie and Sam discover the victim owed his ex-wife thousands of pounds in child support for their terminally ill son and had been lying to his girlfriend about his line of work. As the investigation progresses, the detectives focus their inquiries on the child's grandfather. Did he commit murder to recoup the unpaid money or is there more to this family set-up than first appears? Meanwhile, at the Crown Prosecution Service, Kate and Jake work out their differences. Guest starring Ian Bleasdale (Casualty)(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: A week-long storyline takes place during the opening of Hawkford Outdoor Pursuits. It finishes with Graham's day, and a series of flashbacks revealing the truth(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre takes two weeks off work and cleans the house ready for Samir. Percy encourages Curly to court Paula. Tanya asks Des to drop her from the case(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre fears Samir will have gone off her, but he is thrilled to see her. Vera tells Curly she is disgusted with him leering after Paula. Martin confiscates Nick's video games(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: It is the morning after and the McAllisters wake up to a nightmare. Eric Pollard's plans are threatened by bad publicity, while Ned Glover receives a welcome offer(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The full extent of Luke's predicament is revealed when the McAllisters receive shocking news. Turner struggles alone at the Woolpack, while Ned Glover has something to tell Jan(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Convicted murderer Henry Stoddard's release from prison coincides with a spate of vandalism, and locals presume he is responsible for the petty crime. But when the former jailbird insists he did not kill PC Machin all those years ago, Walker reopens the case and races to stop Machin's son exacting revenge. Meanwhile, Peggy sees the chance to make some money when her birth certificate suggests she is related to Lord Ashfordly(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby invests in some new furniture in a desperate attempt to impress Ruth's replacement, Brenda, but makes the mistake of opting to pay for it on the never-never. Leonard Rossiter and Gay Rose star(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby manages to frighten Alan by kidding him the house is haunted by a spectral 'grey lady' - but ends up getting a taste of his own medicine. Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale star(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin find a rare plant in the Ropers' garden. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective faces a tough time while looking into a murder at a peaceful college. After suffering the familiar pangs of unrequited love, he clashes with the unpleasant college master and then unwittingly provides his main suspects with a watertight alibi. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Robert Stephens, Amanda Burton and Philip Middlemiss(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Local boy Barry Dyson is accused of a series of arson attacks - and his clandestine relationship with a former employer's daughter complicates his plea of innocence. Peggy's latest money-making scheme with old friend Denzil creates an eyesore, forcing Jack to use his artistic flair to rectify matters, before he and Blaketon resort to more drastic - and illegal - measures. Rural drama, starring Gwen Taylor and Derek Fowlds(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Pollard's plotting provokes opposition and Kathy finds a shoulder to cry on. Jan Glover is offered a job, while circumstances cause Luke to lose his freedom(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The outlook for Luke looks grim while his parents run the gauntlet of village gossip. Zoe has a staffing problem and Betty tells all about her summer of love(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick tells Martin he is never going to do anything he says. Mike is stunned to learn that Maggie is getting married. The Robertses go to France(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des worries that Tanya will lie in court, and Mike pressures Alma into letting Mark live with them. Alf gets on well with the French mayor, until Fred throws a spanner in the works(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Larry goes to Athens on a short trip, and returns with a royal guest. Louisa is delighted to have a prince staying under her roof, but soon finds herself with a less welcome visitor in the shape of Captain Creech. Tension is soon stirred up between the two houseguests when Gerry sits an interview for a local school, and the prince and the captain voice very different opinions on the subject of education(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Local boy Barry Dyson is accused of a series of arson attacks - and his clandestine relationship with a former employer's daughter complicates his plea of innocence. Peggy's latest money-making scheme with old friend Denzil creates an eyesore, forcing Jack to use his artistic flair to rectify matters, before he and Blaketon resort to more drastic - and illegal - measures. Rural drama, starring Gwen Taylor and Derek Fowlds(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective's god-daughter Lydia and her son James - who is severely traumatised by a bombing at his school - come to stay unexpectedly. Struggling to look after the lad when his mother goes missing, Foyle's life suffers a further complication when the body of a local man is found near a military centre - leading him into the top-secret world of weapons research. Kate Fleetwood and Michael Jayston guest star, with Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As the sleuth studies for his forthcoming sergeant's exam, he investigates a hit-and-run that claimed the life of an eminent Oxford professor. The victim had been at odds with his peers over the fate of a potentially lucrative parcel of college-owned land, and the case is complicated by the appearance of DI Thursday's old gangland enemy - whom he must face alone while Endeavour makes a trip back to Lincolnshire to visit his ailing father(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Georgia Taylor (Coronation Street, Casualty) and Paterson Joseph (Peep Show) join the cast as the crime drama returns for its seventh series. In the first of a two-part story, a suicidal man causes a train crash when he drives onto a railway crossing, killing 15 people. With Jake prosecuting, the man's defence barrister tries to prove his vulnerability and mental illness, but then a twist in the investigation leaves Sam's career hanging in the balance. Starring Bradley Walsh, Dominic Rowan and Paul Nicholls(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby returns from a dismal holiday in Spain to find a surprise awaiting him - the love of his life, Miss Jones, is back - with a baby in tow. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter(n)

programme.desc: The McAllisters close ranks to support their son. Pollard and Betty make an unlikely partnership, while Zoe has some good news for Linda Glover(n)

programme.desc: The Heritage Farm hosts a summer fair, while Betty's rock concert pulls in the crowds. Seth and Samson save the day for the cricket team, but there is an accident on the way back(n)

programme.desc: Tanya is stunned when Des tells her Alex has settled out of court, and Barnes warns Bet that Tanya is nothing but trouble. The McDonalds entertain Deirdre and Samir(n)

programme.desc: Curly is fed up when Andy receives a glowing management assessment, Jack loses his electricity money on the horses, and Bet attempts to track down Raquel(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Spiros spends time with a glamorous Italian family who have recently arrived on the island. Louisa is jealous of the attention he is paying their new neighbours and invites them over to learn more about them. She ends up convinced they are hiding something, while Margo feels threatened by their eldest daughter. Larry joins the fire service, and Gerry falls in love(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When an RAF officer is found hanged in a wood, Meredith - the new chief superintendent at Hastings - is quick to dismiss it as a straightforward suicide. However, an aerial photo of a German village found in the dead man's pocket leads Milner to suspect murder. The newly retired Foyle is compelled to return to service when events take an even more unexpected turn. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: As darkness covers Midsomer Stanton during a total eclipse of the sun, Jeremy Harper is killed by a blow to the head with a meteorite. The victim was a leading light in the village - an amateur astronomer who was passionate about preserving Moonstone Ridge, a Bronze Age heritage site housing a 1930s observatory. Barnaby and Jones are called in to investigate, and the duo discover that intrigue, sexual tension and academic rivalry are rife among the local stargazing community. Murder mystery, guest starring Maureen Lipman and Kenny Ireland, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sam is suspended from duty and decides to spend some time with his son, while Brooks and Leyton make a breakthrough when they discover who was responsible for the train crash and put the culprit under surveillance. Jake and Kate argue over whether it was right to prosecute Finn Tyler in his fragile mental state. Drama, starring Paul Nicholls and Bradley Walsh(n)
